input.command: cd /mydata/karma; for f in cases/ray/deploy_cluster/resource/ray-head.yaml cases/ray/deploy_cluster/resource/ray-head-service.yaml cases/ray/deploy_cluster/resource/ray-worker.yaml; do echo "===== $f ====="; cat "$f"; done

content.content:
===== cases/ray/deploy_cluster/resource/ray-head.yaml =====
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: ray-head
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
    app.kubernetes.io/component: head
    bench.ray.cluster: ray
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
      app.kubernetes.io/component: head
      bench.ray.cluster: ray
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
        app.kubernetes.io/component: head
        bench.ray.cluster: ray
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: ray-head
        image: rayproject/ray:2.9.0
        env:
          - name: MY_POD_IP
            valueFrom:
              fieldRef:
                fieldPath: status.podIP
          - name: RAY_NODE_ID
            valueFrom:
              fieldRef:
                fieldPath: metadata.uid
          - name: RAY_RAYLET_PID
            value: "1"
        command:
          - sh
          - -c
          - |
            ray start --head --node-ip-address="${MY_POD_IP}" --port=6379 --include-dashboard=false --num-cpus=0 --block
        ports:
          - containerPort: 6379
            name: gcs
===== cases/ray/deploy_cluster/resource/ray-head-service.yaml =====
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: ray-head
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
    app.kubernetes.io/component: head-service
    bench.ray.cluster: ray
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
    app.kubernetes.io/component: head
    bench.ray.cluster: ray
  ports:
    - name: gcs
      port: 6379
      targetPort: 6379
===== cases/ray/deploy_cluster/resource/ray-worker.yaml =====
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: ray-worker
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
    app.kubernetes.io/component: worker
    bench.ray.cluster: ray
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
      app.kubernetes.io/component: worker
      bench.ray.cluster: ray
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: ray
        app.kubernetes.io/component: worker
        bench.ray.cluster: ray
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: ray-worker
        image: rayproject/ray:2.9.0
        env:
          - name: MY_POD_IP
            valueFrom:
              fieldRef:
                fieldPath: status.podIP
          - name: RAY_NODE_ID
            valueFrom:
              fieldRef:
                fieldPath: metadata.uid
          - name: RAY_RAYLET_PID
            value: "1"
        command:
          - sh
          - -c
          - |
            ray start --address=ray-head:6379 --node-ip-address="${MY_POD_IP}" --num-cpus=1 --block
Shell cwd was reset to /mydata/karma/runs/error-prone-rerun/ray-hardening-chain-job-change-plan-audit-20260618_092129/stages/stage_01

content.text: Validation failed — the client can't connect. Let me investigate the head and client.

input.command:
echo "=== head logs ==="; kubectl -n ray logs deployment/ray-head --tail=30 2>&1
echo "=== client ray version ==="; kubectl -n ray exec ray-client -- python -c "import ray; print(ray.__version__)" 2>&1 | tail -2
echo "=== head ray version ==="; kubectl -n ray exec deployment/ray-head -- ray --version 2>&1 | tail -2

content.text: Same error. `ray.init(address=...)` connects a driver to an **existing local raylet** — it doesn't start one. So ray-client must already be a Ray node. Let me check what ray-client is actually running.

"}{.spec.containers[0].args}{"
"}'
echo "=== processes in ray-client ==="; kubectl -n ray exec ray-client -- ps aux 2>&1 | grep -i ray | grep -v grep
echo "=== ray-client logs ==="; kubectl -n ray logs ray-client --tail=20 2>&1
